title: Arcstep Horde
hypothesis: Combining familiar side-view movement and forgiving jumping with automatic aimed attacks will make horde evasion and platform positioning immediately fun.
preserve: side-view platform movement with Arrow Keys or A/D
Space or Up jumping with forgiving coyote time and jump buffering
automatic aimed combat against an escalating enemy swarm
defeat drops that visibly grow automatic weapon power
the objective's three-to-five-minute target session, represented by a compressed 96-second escalation demo

provenance.report: Tests the readable baseline: move and jump across a compact six-platform arena while bolts automatically aim at the nearest threats. Defeated enemies drop magnetic sparks; each power tier visibly enlarges the aura, adds an orbiting spark, accelerates fire, and periodically adds another aimed bolt. The horde spawns faster and adds heavier jumpers over a compressed 96-second demonstration. The smallest useful playtest measurement is whether the player reaches the first power tier and keeps using jumps to reposition rather than waiting on one platform.
